title: PV Sindhu Ends Title Drought, Wins Maiden Japan Open Crown
indian_angle: Indian badminton star PV Sindhu secured her first title since 2024, marking a significant return to form by winning the Japan Open.
summary: PV Sindhu defeated Akane Yamaguchi 21-17, 21-17 in 50 minutes to win the Japan Open 2026, her first title since the Syed Modi India International in 2024.

title: Delhi World Championships Venue Revamped After India Open Issues
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has confirmed significant upgrades to the Delhi venue for the BWF World Championships 2026, addressing past issues like stray animals and facilities.
summary: The BAI general secretary announced that the venue for the BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi has undergone major renovations, including adding doors to prevent animal entry and upgrading facilities, following embarrassments at the India Open.

title: Gopichand Praises Sindhu's Resurgence, Sees Threat to Top Players
indian_angle: National coach Pullela Gopichand believes PV Sindhu's recent form indicates she can once again challenge and defeat top international players.
summary: Pullela Gopichand commented on PV Sindhu's renewed form, stating that she has the capability to beat top players, hinting at her potential for the World Championships and LA 2028.

title: World Junior Championships 2026 Selection Criteria and Tournament Changes
indian_angle: Recent BAI circulars detail eligibility and selection tournament changes for Indian players aiming for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo.
summary: BAI has issued circulars regarding player eligibility for the World Junior Championships 2026 and announced the postponement of the Haryana U-19 Ranking Tournament, declaring the Bhubaneswar U-19 event as the new selection tournament.

title: BAI Implements 3x15 Scoring System for Junior Domestic Events
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has introduced a new 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and national championships.
summary: The BAI has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age categories, effective from June 30, 2026.

title: Grassroot Coaches Development Program at NCE Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: BAI is organizing a Coaches Development Program at the Grassroot Level in Guwahati and Raipur, aiming to enhance coaching standards across India.
summary: The BAI has circulated information for a Coaches Development Program at the Grassroot Level, scheduled to take place at National Centre of Excellence (NCE) Guwahati & Raipur from July 15th to 24th, 2026.
